author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “to the end” means, must not be too often repeated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth;” as the Apostle saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We believe on Him, when we begin to enter on the good road: we shall see Him, when we shall get to the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore is He the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not even have certain sacrilegious and abominable philosophers, who entertain perverse and false notions of God, dared to say, “There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it is, hath said “in his heart;” for that no one dares to say it, even if he has dared to think it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They are corrupt, and become abominable in their affections:” that is, whilst they love this world and love not God; these are the affections which corrupt the soul, and so blind it, that the fool can even say, “in his heart, There is no God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is none that doeth goodness, no not up to one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Up to one,” can be understood either with that one, so that no man be understood: or besides one, that the Lord Christ may be excepted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As we say, This field is up to the sea; we do not of course reckon the sea together with the field.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is the better interpretation, so that none be understood to have done goodness up to Christ; for that no man can do goodness, except He shall have shown it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that is true; for until a man know the one God, he cannot do goodness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord from heaven looked out upon the sons of men, to see if there be one understanding, or seeking after God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It may be interpreted, upon the Jews; as he may have given them the more honourable name of the sons of men, by reason of their worship of the One God, in comparison with the Gentiles; of whom I suppose it was said above, “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God,” etc. Now the Lord looks out, that He may see, by His holy souls: which is the meaning of, “from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by Himself nothing is hid from Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All have gone out of the way, they have together become useless:” that is, the Jews have become as the Gentiles, who were spoken of above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is none that doeth good, no not up to one” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3), must be interpreted as above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their throat is an open sepulchre.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Either the voracity of the ever open palate is signified: or allegorically those who slay, and as it were devour those they have slain, into whom they instil the disorder of their own conversation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Like to which with the contrary meaning is that which was said to Peter, “Kill and eat;” that he should convert the Gentiles to his own faith and good conversation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With their tongues they have dealt craftily.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Flattery is the companion of the greedy and of all bad men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The poison of asps is under their lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “poison,” he means deceit; and “of asps,” because they will not hear the precepts of the law, as asps “will not hear the voice of the charmer;” which is said more clearly in another Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:” this is, “the poison of asps.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their feet are swift to shed blood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He here shows forth the habit of ill doing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Destruction and unhappiness” are “in their ways.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all the ways of evil men are full of toil and misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence the Lord cries out, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will refresh you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take My yoke upon you, and learn of Me, for I am meek and lowly in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For My yoke is easy and My burden light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the way of peace have they not known:” that way, namely, which the Lord, as I said, mentions, in the easy yoke and light burden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These do not say, “There is no God;” but yet they do not fear God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not all, who work iniquity, know?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He threatens the judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who devour My people as the food of bread:” that is, daily.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the food of bread is daily food.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they devour the people, who serve their own ends out of them, not referring their ministry to the glory of God, and the salvation of those over whom they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have not called upon the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he doth not really call upon Him, who longs for such things as are displeasing to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There they trembled for fear, where no fear was” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5): that is, for the loss of things temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they said, “If we let Him thus alone, all men will believe on Him; and the Romans will come, and take away both our place and nation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They feared to lose an earthly kingdom, where no fear was; and they lost the kingdom of heaven, which they ought to have feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this must be understood of all temporal goods, the loss of which when men fear, they come not to things eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God is in the just generation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It refers to what went before, so that the sense is, “shall not all they that work iniquity know that the Lord is in the just generation;” that is, He is not in them who love the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is unjust to leave the Maker of the worlds, and “serve the creature more than the Creator.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, for the Lord is his hope” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6): that is, ye have despised the humble coming of the Son of God, because ye saw not in Him the pomp of the world: that they, whom he was calling, should put their hope in God alone, not in the things that pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who will give salvation to Israel out of Sion?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who but He whose humiliation ye have despised? is understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He will come in glory to the judgment of the quick and the dead, and the kingdom of the just: that, forasmuch as in that humble coming “blindness hath happened in part unto Israel, that the fulness of the Gentiles might enter in,” in that other should happen what follows, “and so all Israel should be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostle too takes that testimony of Isaiah, where it is said, “There shall come out of Sion He who shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:” for the Jews, as it is here, “Who shall give salvation to Israel out of Sion?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the Lord shall turn away the captivity of His people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a repetition, as is usual: for I suppose, “Israel shall be glad,” is the same as, “Jacob shall rejoice.”
